title: Error: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1) when running container using Podman
body:
### Issue Description

Hi

I tried to run container in Docker container (Alpine Linux) using Podman however received an error described below.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create Dockerfile:

```
FROM alpine
VOLUME /var/lib/containers
RUN apk add podman 
```

Build and run this container using Docker, then in the console type command: **podman run hello-world**

### Describe the results you received

```
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f20be95f9e7e done   |
Copying config 671dfeee36 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1)
```

### Describe the results you expected

Hello-world container should be run successfully as Alpine Linux is officially supported in Podman documentation.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
How is the outer container created? Nested container are in general complex to setup.

Can you run the podman command with `--log-level debug` and show the full output.

Hi @Luap99 

I investigated this issue a lot and it seems to me that Podman always requires --privileged flag to run inside Docker container (docker run --privileged). Otherwise I would face different permission errors.
If I want to run Podman container during Docker build I have to use Buildkit features (--security=insecure):
https://docs.docker.com/reference/dockerfile/#run---security

also see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container
I assume this is good to close then
